$comment: Hand-maintained curation overrides for the Fungrim Phase-1 rule compiler (FUNGRIM-PLAN-5-LOADER.md §2.3/§2.6). Merged last by scripts/fungrim/compile-rules.ts. `overrides` entries ({ direction?, purpose?, target?, exclude?, note? }, keyed by corpus entry id) adjust or exclude compiled artifact rules. `transformAllowlist` promotes hand-vetted growth-neutral canonicalizations from 'expand' to 'transform' (the machine policy never emits 'transform'). `inject` is a list of hand-curated corpus-Entry-shaped synthetic entries appended to the slice and compiled through the FULL pipeline (guards, orientation, self-test) — for sound identities missing from the upstream corpus. `solveSeeds` is the §2.6 hand-curated solve-template seed set: it is NOT compiled into the M1 artifact and does not affect default loading — the M2 runtime loader compiles these entries into UNIVARIATE_ROOTS-style templates behind loadIdentities(ce, { solve: true }) (off by default). PHASE 2 ACTIVATED: the separate post-step scripts/fungrim/apply-solve-templates.ts derives a UNIVARIATE_ROOTS-style root template from each seed's emitted inverse-composition simplify rule and appends it as a target:'solve' rule (id fungrim:<id>:solve); run it AFTER any compile-rules.ts run. recompileDivergence is the allowlist for scripts/fungrim/recompile-drift.ts (the no-silent-drops gate).

304559.note: Phase 3 triage: nondeterministic self-test (random-probe isEqual over Coulomb shell expressions) — see 01af55.

5bd0ec.note: Phase 3 triage: ChebyshevT(n, (x + 1/x)/2) -> (x^n + x^-n)/2 is a sound recognition rule, but its (x + 1/x)/2 argument pattern matches DEGENERATE literals (1 = (1 + 1/1)/2 with x = 1), rewriting ChebyshevT(n, 1) to (1^n + 1^-n)/2 inside simplify() and shadowing the direct value rule fc5d42 (CE does not fold 1^n for symbolic n). Demoted to 'expand': reachable via replace(), out of simplify()'s scan.

01af55.note: Phase 3 triage: self-test fired-vs-expected comparison is nondeterministic — the result settles in a different canonical shape and the isEqual fallback random-probes deep CoulombF/G/Sigma shell expressions, flipping across runs (breaks artifact byte-determinism). Excluded pending shell-aware structural comparison.

2a2f18.note: Phase 3 triage: nondeterministic self-test (random-probe isEqual over Coulomb shell expressions) — see 01af55.

d280c5.note: Phase 3 triage: nondeterministic self-test (random-probe isEqual over Coulomb shell expressions) — see 01af55.

177de7.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(1/6) specific value. Its closed form (a few Ln/Pi/Sqrt terms) is larger than the tiny Digamma(1/6) input, so simplify()'s 1.3x cost gate rejected it and the rule was a no-op. A specific value's closed form is always the desired answer, so tag 'transform' (cost-gate exempt).

45a969.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(2/3) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

7ec4f0.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(1/4) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

89bed3.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(1/2) -> -2 ln 2 - EulerGamma. The flagged rule: cost-gated no-op, now 'transform' (cost-gate exempt). See 177de7.

8c368f.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(1/8) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

967bbb.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(5/6) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

98f642.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(1/3) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

f93bae.note: SYM P2-25: Digamma(3/4) specific value — cost-gated no-op, see 177de7.

inject.references: Curated injection (not in the upstream corpus slice): the trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function, Zeta(-2n) = 0 for positive integer n. The Track-3 guard machinery discharges the positive-integer guard for symbolic n (declared integer + assumed n > 0); literal arguments are covered by the exact Zeta evaluate path in library/arithmetic.ts.

296627.note: Exp(Ln(z)) = z inverse composition. complex-domain guard accepted for solve templates because validateRoots filters every candidate against the original equation.

8654a3.note: W(x*e^x) = x — seed for the LambertW root template _x*Exp(_x) + __b = 0 -> _x = LambertW(Negate(__b)). Off by default; validateRoots checks every candidate against the original equation, so an over-broad template degrades to a no-op, never a wrong answer.

ed7dac.note: LambertW branch -1 variant of 8654a3 (2-arg LambertW). Trips the compat-signature gate (CE's LambertW is 1-arg principal branch) so its simplify rule is excluded from the artifact; apply-solve-templates.ts therefore reports it as an UNAVAILABLE seed (no :solve rule emitted) — revisit when branch support lands (Phase 2).

4c1e1e.note: Ln(Exp(z)) = z inverse composition. complex-domain guard accepted for solve templates because validateRoots filters every candidate against the original equation (the safety property promised by the ce.solveRules docs).

1f026d.note: Tan(Arctan(z)) = z inverse composition. complex-domain guard accepted for solve templates because validateRoots filters every candidate against the original equation.

f516e3.note: Arctan(Tan(theta)) = theta inverse composition (principal branch). complex-domain guard accepted for solve templates because validateRoots filters every candidate against the original equation.

solveTemplates.note: Hand-curated (not corpus-derivable): c*p^(m*x) + a*x + b = 0 solves to x = -b/a - W((m*ln(p)*c/a)*p^(-m*b/a))/(m*ln(p)), the general LambertW root of a linear-plus-single-exponential. Handles shapes with an explicit linear coefficient and/or constant (e.g. e^x - x - 2 = 0). validateRoots checks every candidate against the original equation, so an over-broad match degrades to a no-op, never a wrong root. The all-coefficients-absent shape (p^x + x = 0) is handled by the companion lambertw-exp-bare template because useVariations does not synthesize every missing __c/__m/__a/__b slot simultaneously.

Hand-curated companion to lambertw-linear-exp for the bare shape p^x + x = 0 (no exponential coefficient, no exponent scale, unit linear term, no constant), which solves to x = -W(ln(p))/ln(p). Split out because useVariations cannot synthesize the missing __c=1, __m=1, __a=1, __b=0 slots of the master template simultaneously. Covers e^x + x = 0 and x + 2^x = 0. Soundness guaranteed downstream by validateRoots.

Branch -1 (second real branch) companion of lambertw-linear-exp: c*p^(m*x) + a*x + b = 0 also solves to x = -b/a - W_{-1}((m*ln(p)*c/a)*p^(-m*b/a))/(m*ln(p)) wherever the W argument lands in [-1/e, 0). Emits the SECOND real root of a linear-plus-single-exponential (e.g. the x = 1.146... root of e^x - x - 2 = 0). W_{-1} returns NaN outside its domain, so validateRoots drops the candidate for shapes with a single real root (never a wrong root).

Branch -1 companion of lambertw-exp-bare for the bare shape p^x + x = 0: x = -W_{-1}(ln(p))/ln(p). Real only when ln(p) in [-1/e, 0), i.e. p in [e^{-1/e}, 1); for p >= 1 (e.g. e^x + x = 0, x + 2^x = 0) the W argument ln(p) >= 0 falls outside W_{-1}'s domain and the NaN candidate is dropped by validateRoots (those equations have a single real root). Tested at p = 0.8.

recompileDivergence.$comment: Allowlist for scripts/fungrim/recompile-drift.ts -- the 'no silent drops' gate. The gate full-recompiles the slice and FAILS if any committed simplify rule would be dropped (or any new rule added) without an entry here, so a future silent drop/add is impossible. The artifact is currently FULLY REPRODUCIBLE from compile-rules.ts (the self-test's sign-neutral fallback recovers the rules that only fire within the full rule set), so both lists are EMPTY -- any nonzero divergence is a real signal to investigate (a self-test false-negative to keep, a dead rule to drop, or a missing rule to add). Keyed by bare entry id.
